what_it_does: Implements a stepped, adaptive extraction pipeline that reads transcript chunks from a substrate SQLite database, runs a cheap coarse extraction on every chunk, gates to a richer fine extraction only when the coarse kind is decision/spec/discussion, optionally adds a visual-dna design stage, and writes the merged records as JSONL to .data/store/extractions/. Supports sample/proof runs and a resume-capable full bake.
observations: Uses a two-stage cascade: coarse on all chunks, then a step-gate to fine only for kinds in {decision, spec, discussion}.
Optionally runs a third DESIGN stage over every chunk when --design is set, merging resolves_into and resolution fields.
Loads prompts/roles from a discovered role registry rather than hard-coding them, caching the registry in a global.
Guards chunk text with a leading space in _safe_item so run_items treats it as a literal rather than an address.
Parses conversation date from the chunk anchor string, not from file mtime.
Filters projects by exact leading path segment and supports an additional --path-prefix substring filter.
Full bake mode writes incrementally in 500-record batches and can resume from an existing JSONL file by chunk_id.
The full bake is gated by a printed checklist and requires --confirm, but the script does not programmatically verify the checklist items.
Defaults coarse_max=220 and fine_max=600, with throughput and truncation concerns documented in the module docstring.
load_chunks truncates each chunk text to 600 characters before returning it.
standouts: The step-gate is forward-only and decided from the coarse output at extraction time, avoiding re-extraction or up-projection.
_safe_item is a small, file-local defense against a real failure mode where transcript examples starting with a registered scheme were misclassified as addresses.
Resume-by-chunk_id makes the long full bake crash-tolerant rather than all-or-nothing.
conventions: Heavy module-level docstring explaining design rationale, source, output, budget, and gate checklist.
Uses type hints and from __future__ annotations.
Fail-loud style: missing registry row raises KeyError, address guard mutates rather than skips.
Separates schema (contracts.dragnet_schema), roles (runtime RoleRegistry), and orchestration (this file).
concerns: The docstring says it records finish_reason so truncation is visible, but the code does not store finish_reason in the output records.
The docstring mentions gating on coarse signals claim-bearing, but the gate code only checks the kind field.
load_chunks returns text[:600], which may discard most of a chunk before fine extraction even though fine_max=600 tokens could handle far more text.
Resume relies on chunk_id uniqueness and stable ordering; appending to the same file across restarts could produce duplicates if chunk_ids are reused or if the filter changes.
_safe_item silently prepends a space to the literal text, which may affect tokenization or model behavior for those few chunks.
The full-bake gate checklist is only printed; the script does not enforce throughput measurement, token sizing, or filter presence before running.
notes: A staged LLM extraction orchestrator that tries to spend model budget only where the coarse stage says a chunk is substantive, with file-local guards for address misclassification and crash-safe resume.
questions: What are the exact field shapes of Coarse, Fine, and Design imported from contracts.dragnet_schema?
How does run_items classify a unit as an address, and which schemes are registered?
Where is finish_reason actually captured and stored?
How is the 32-concurrent throughput measurement supposed to be performed before the full bake?
Why is chunk text truncated to 600 characters in load_chunks?
What distinguishes the visual-dna vault such that the design stage must run on every chunk rather than being gated?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly aligned: it is a stepped coarse-to-fine extractor with resume and sample modes. However, the stated finish_reason recording and claim-bearing gate are not implemented, and the full-bake gate checklist is advisory rather than enforced.
apparent_themes: Adaptive depth and budget-aware LLM pipelines
Separation of prompts/roles via a registry
Literal-vs-address safety in a typed item runner
Provenance and date fidelity over file metadata
intention_signals: The file is trying to materialize a single, reusable extraction asset where each chunk is processed no more deeply than necessary, while making truncation visible and long runs recoverable; it also tries to prevent accidental address resolution of transcript examples.
novelty: The combination of a coarse-output step-gate, an address-classification guard for literal transcript text, registry-driven dragnet roles, and an additive design stage for a specific vault.
contribution: A runnable orchestrator that turns substrate chunks into merged coarse/fine/design JSONL records, with filtering, sampling, batched incremental output, and resume support, plus a concrete guard against address misclassification.
summary_for_embedding: ops/dragnet_extract.py is a Python orchestrator that performs staged LLM extraction on transcript chunks from a substrate database, producing coarse/fine/design JSONL records with adaptive depth, filtering, and resume support.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to build the #65 extract-once dragnet asset efficiently and safely, avoiding over-extraction and making long batch runs recoverable.

symbols.does: Loads and caches the discovered role registry, then returns the requested dragnet role row by id.
symbols.description: A helper that lazily discovers roles under the repo's roles/ directory using RoleRegistry and caches the result in a module global. It is the single access point for the dragnet coarse, fine, and design roles, and fails loudly with a KeyError if a protected role is missing.

symbols.does: Returns the dragnet_coarse role from the registry.
symbols.description: Thin wrapper around _dragnet_role('dragnet_coarse') that isolates the coarse-stage prompt lookup.

symbols.does: Returns the dragnet_fine role from the registry.
symbols.description: Thin wrapper around _dragnet_role('dragnet_fine') that isolates the fine-stage prompt lookup.

symbols.does: Returns the dragnet_design role from the registry.
symbols.description: Thin wrapper around _dragnet_role('dragnet_design') that isolates the optional visual-dna design-stage prompt lookup.

symbols.does: Reads filtered transcript chunks from a substrate SQLite database in read-only mode.
symbols.description: Queries the chunks and addresses tables, applies project, vault, path-prefix, date-range, size, sample-step, and limit filters, and returns a list of chunk dictionaries. Conversation date is parsed from the chunk anchor string rather than file mtime; chunk text is truncated to 600 characters.

symbols.does: Prepends a space to chunk text that starts with a registered address scheme.
symbols.description: A guard to ensure run_items treats transcript literals as literals instead of resolving them as addresses. Only the few chunks that begin with a registered scheme are modified; the rest pass through unchanged.

symbols.does: Runs the stepped coarse/fine/design extraction over a list of chunks and returns merged records plus stats.
symbols.description: Executes coarse extraction on every chunk, selects gated indices whose kind is decision/spec/discussion, runs fine extraction only on those indices, optionally runs design extraction on every chunk, and merges all outputs into superset records. Returns the records and timing/count statistics.

symbols.does: Parses CLI arguments, loads chunks, runs extraction, and writes JSONL output.
symbols.description: Handles sample runs, gated full-bake resume with 500-record batched appends, and proof-only sample output. It sets up an FsStore, invokes run_extract, prints stats, and writes records to .data/store/extractions/ when requested.
